rulings.Case Title: LOVELL WHITMAN VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al

PARTIES TO LODGE SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATIONS AND PROPOSED ORDERS. NO APPEARANCES NECESSARY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: AURORA LAGRONE VS. LAW OFFICE OF MAYOR JOSEPH ALIOTO L. ALIOTO &. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Strike All Or Part Of Complaint, Request For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICE OF MAYOR JOSEPH L. ALIOTO, PAMELA PITT AND ANGELA ALIOTO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (Request For Judicial NOTICE) IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBITS C THROUGH F, H AND I IS DENIED UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452(H) TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANTS SEEK TO HAVE THE CONTENTS OF THE DOCUMENTS ACCEPTED FOR THE TRUTH OF THE MATTER STATED. UNDER CHARNAY V COBERT (2006) 145 CAL. APP. 4TH 170, THE COURT MAY NOT DETERMINE ISSUES OF FACT WHEN RULING ON A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE UNFAIR COMPETITION IS DISMISSED BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S NON-OPPOSITION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL FRANCIS PELOSI VS. GARY ROSSI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution And Ot Enforce The Settlement Agreement;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2008 LINE 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT GARY ROSSI'S Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution And TO Enforce The Settlement Agreement: THE MOTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE COURT HAS INHERENT POWER UNDER MEYER V MEYER (1952) 115 CAL. APP. 2D 48 TO QUASH A WRIT, THE EXERCISE OF SUCH POWER WOULD REQUIRE THE COURT TO ANALYZE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO DETERMINE WHETHER THERE WAS A BREACH. AS A CONSEQUENCE, IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT C.C.P. SECTION 664.6 CONTROLS AND LACKS JURISDICTION TO DECIDE THIS ISSUE. THIS DOES NOT PRECLUDE ROSSI FROM FILING A SEPARATE ACTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2008 LINE 8. rulings.Case Title: RAMON ZAMUDIO VS. THOMAS BYUN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANT'S THOMAS BYUN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION THREE, FIVE AND NINE WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BASED ON THE ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, C.C.P. 340 (1). MENEFEE V OSTAWARI (1991) 228 CAL. APP. 3D 239, 243 THROUGH 244. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN FIVE DAYS AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION SIX, SEVEN, EIGHT, TEN AND ELEVEN IN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE A DUTY OF CARE APART FROM LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP. SUCH A RELATIONSHIP DOES NOT CREATE AN INDEPENDENT DUTY OF CARE. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: KEITH BROWN VS. THE TJX COMPANIES INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike; Demurrer To Answer Of The Tjx Companies, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2008 LINE 12. PLAINTIFF KEITH BROWN'S Motion To Strike; Demurrer To Answer Of The T.J.X. Companies, Inc.: MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. T.J.X. HAS THE RIGHT TO DEFEND THE ACTION. DEMURRER TO THE ANSWER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FOLLOWING DEFENSES WHICH REQUIRE THE PLEADING OF FACTS. 5 WITKIN, CAL. PROC. 2D (4TH ED.) PLEADING SECTION 1008: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, AND 17. TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. OVERRULED AS TO 1, 2, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 AND 16. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND 18 AND 19. =(302/PJM/X-MS)
